artifacts.name: yolo-jail Python wheel/sdist (console scripts: yolo=src.cli:main, yolo-claude-oauth-broker-host=src.oauth_broker:main, yolo-host-processes=src.host_processes:main, yolo-ps=src.yolo_ps:main)
artifacts.built_how: `uv build` (Justfile `build`, rm -rf dist/ first); setuptools>=64 + setuptools-scm>=8 backend; version dynamic from git tags -> src/_version.py ([tool.setuptools-scm] version_file; also mirrored under [tool.vcs-versioning]); packages = [src, src.bundled_loopholes, src.cli, src.entrypoint]; package-data ships src/flake.nix + src/flake.lock (SYMLINKS to repo root, resolved at build), src/shims/*, and src/bundled_loopholes/**/* inside the wheel — the installed CLI runs `nix build` against the bundled flake (run_cmd.py copies flake.nix/flake.lock to a build root, mtime-compared)
artifacts.shipped_how: 3 channels: (1) `just install` -> `uv tool install --force dist/*.whl` locally; (2) publish.yml on GitHub release -> `uv publish` to PyPI (OIDC/id-token, environment: pypi, --check-url); (3) `brew` job regenerates a Homebrew formula via homebrew-pypi-poet (pinned setuptools<81), patches desc/homepage/license/python@3.13/test block in inline Python, pushes to mschulkind-oss/homebrew-tap with HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN; workflow_dispatch with version input re-runs brew only (PyPI must already have the version)

artifacts.name: ociImage (full jail image, yolo-jail:latest) / ociImageMinimal (yolo-jail:ci-minimal)
artifacts.built_how: flake.nix mkOciImage via pkgs.dockerTools.streamLayeredImage (aliased ociTools; maxLayers 100, created=now, Cmd=/bin/bash, WorkingDir=/workspace, Env sets PATH=${shims}/bin:/bin:/usr/bin, SSL_CERT_FILE, LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/lib:/usr/lib:/usr/lib/<multilib>, PKG_CONFIG_PATH, FONTCONFIG_*, TZDIR). contents = binPathLinks(Minimal) + corePackages (bash, coreutils-full, git, rg, fd, curl, cacert, mise, node22, python3, gh, uv, iptables, socat, sox, overmind, tzdata, jq, procps, archive tools) + fullPackages (chromium, gcc, nix, podman, fuse-overlayfs, slirp4netns, shadow, openssh, strace, tmux, etc., ~1.6-2GB, omitted in minimal) + extraPackages from YOLO_EXTRA_PACKAGES env (JSON via builtins.getEnv -> requires --impure; string 'pkg'/'pkg.dev' or objects {name,nixpkgs}|{name,version,url,hash}|{name,outputs}). Also embeds: shims derivation (src/shims/* copied to bin/), entrypointPkg (cp -r src/entrypoint -> /lib/python/entrypoint), entrypoint wrapper (pkgs.writeTextFile, shebang hardcoded to imagePkgs.bashInteractive Linux store path; execs imagePkgs.python313 -m entrypoint with PYTHONPATH=<entrypointPkg>/lib/python), yoloCli in-jail wrapper (/bin/yolo -> `uv run --no-project --with typer/rich/pyjson5 python3 -c "from src.cli import main; main()"` against live-mounted /opt/yolo-jail). darwin->linux mapping: imageSystem = replaceStrings -darwin -linux, imagePkgs used for all image contents so a Mac host needs a Linux builder (or Cachix). fakeRootCommands pre-create mountpoints (/home/agent /workspace /opt/yolo-jail /ctx/* /nix/var/nix/daemon-socket) for --read-only rootfs and write /etc/passwd,/etc/group
artifacts.shipped_how: Never pushed to a registry as the jail image: flake output is a STREAMER script; `just load` = `nix build .#ociImage && ./result | $YOLO_RUNTIME load` (runtime := env YOLO_RUNTIME, default podman). CI builds minimal variant with `nix build --impure .#ociImageMinimal`, tars it, passes as artifact to the integration job which `podman load`s it. Planned: `just cachix-push` / publish.yml push-image-cache job push both variants' nix closures to Cachix on release (gated on CACHIX_AUTH_TOKEN secret + CACHIX_CACHE var; currently no-op, nixConfig substituter block in flake commented out) so macOS hosts substitute instead of building

artifacts.name: builderImage (yolo-jail-builder — nix+sshd Linux remote builder for macOS container runtimes)
artifacts.built_how: flake output packages.builderImage, streamLayeredImage with imagePkgs (Linux target): nix, openssh, bashInteractive, coreutils, cacert + generated /etc (passwd/group with root+builder+sshd, nix.conf with trusted-users, sandbox=false). sshd_config forces `nix-daemon --stdio` (ssh-ng protocol), key-only root login, no TTY/forwarding. KEYLESS image: authorized key injected at run time via YOLO_BUILDER_PUBKEY env; entrypoint shell script generates host key on first boot and execs sshd -D
artifacts.shipped_how: publish.yml push-builder-image job on release: native ubuntu-24.04-arm runner builds .#packages.aarch64-linux.builderImage with nix build --impure, streams to /tmp/builder.tar, skopeo copy docker-archive -> ghcr.io/<owner>/yolo-jail-builder:{VERSION,latest}; then best-effort gh api PATCH to flip package visibility public. aarch64-linux only (Apple Silicon audience)

artifacts.name: shims derivation (yolo-shims)
artifacts.built_how: trivial stdenv.mkDerivation over src/shims/ — cp * to $out/bin, chmod +x; built on host system (pkgs, not imagePkgs — plain text scripts)
artifacts.shipped_how: baked into image contents; ${shims}/bin is FIRST in image PATH so shims shadow real binaries; same scripts also ship in the wheel as package-data src/shims/*

artifacts.name: binPathLinks / binPathLinksMinimal (FHS shim layer)
artifacts.built_how: pkgs.runCommand mkBinPathLinks {withChromium, withNestedPodman}: symlinks /usr/bin/env, /bin/{bash,sh,awk,sed,grep,find}, dynamic linker at /lib{,64}, lib*.so symlink farm into /lib+/usr/lib (glibc/gcc-lib/zlib + getLib of every user package spec + chromium graphics stack), chromium aliases (chromium/google-chrome/chrome), fonts, /etc/containers configs for nested rootless podman (subuid/subgid, storage.conf w/ fuse-overlayfs, containers.conf, policy.json, registries.conf), /etc/ld.so.conf + /etc/ld.so.cache->/run symlink (cache generated at container start by entrypoint, NOT build time, because darwin hosts can't run Linux ldconfig), /etc/localtime+timezone -> /run tmpfs. Exposed standalone as packages.binPathLinks (minimal variant) so tests can assert lib discovery without an image build
artifacts.shipped_how: as first element of image contents; standalone flake output for fast test builds

artifacts.name: yoloDarwinPackages (macos-user backend profile) + darwinUnavailablePackages
artifacts.built_how: pkgs.buildEnv over darwinPackages: same YOLO_EXTRA_PACKAGES specs resolved natively against aarch64-darwin pkgs with warn-and-skip (`?` attr guard + tryEval availableOn; pinned-nixpkgs/version-override fetch failures NOT catchable — abort whole eval); extraOutputsToInstall [bin lib dev]. darwinUnavailablePackages = skipped names (plain eval output, not a derivation)
artifacts.shipped_how: realized on the Mac host by src/cli/darwin_packages.py via `nix build --print-out-paths`; <out>/bin goes on the sandboxed agent PATH; never containerized

pipelines.name: .github/workflows/ci.yml
pipelines.what: push/PR to main. Jobs: secrets-scan (trufflehog --only-verified); check (ubuntu: uv sync --group dev, ruff check, ruff format --check, pytest -m 'not slow' -n 4 --dist worksteal); check-macos (macos-latest, same minus format-check + integration, gated to same-repo PRs); build-image (matrix ubuntu-latest + ubuntu-24.04-arm, needs check: free ~25GB disk, install-nix-action@v31, `nix build --impure .#ociImageMinimal`, stream ./result > tar, upload artifact jail-image-<os>, retention 1 day); integration (same matrix, needs build-image: free disk, uv sync, nix, apt install podman socat, download+`podman load` the tar, `uv run pytest tests/test_jail.py -v` with YOLO_RUNTIME=podman). Workflow-wide FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24=true. Note: --impure is required because the flake reads YOLO_EXTRA_PACKAGES via builtins.getEnv

pipelines.name: .github/workflows/publish.yml
pipelines.what: on release published (+ workflow_dispatch w/ version input for brew-only backfill). Jobs: build (uv build -> dist artifact); publish (environment pypi, id-token: write, `uv publish dist/* --check-url`); brew (always() gate: generates Homebrew formula with homebrew-pypi-poet against the PyPI package — retries pip install 6x15s for index lag — patches placeholders inline, commits Formula/yolo-jail.rb to mschulkind-oss/homebrew-tap); push-image-cache (matrix x86_64+arm, no-op unless CACHIX_AUTH_TOKEN + CACHIX_CACHE configured; nix build --impure both image variants under cachix-action which auto-pushes closures); push-builder-image (ubuntu-24.04-arm, packages: write; skopeo login GHCR w/ GITHUB_TOKEN, build .#packages.aarch64-linux.builderImage, skopeo copy docker-archive tar to ghcr.io/<owner>/yolo-jail-builder:VERSION+latest, best-effort gh api to make package public)

pipelines.name: .github/workflows/nightly-macos.yml
pipelines.what: cron 06:00 UTC + dispatch. build-image on ubuntu (FULL .#ociImage this time) -> artifact; integration-macos on macos-26-intel (Intel for nested-virt Podman Machine; Apple Silicon runners lack it; Intel images retire ~late 2027): install nix + uv, install podman 5.8.4 via pinned upstream .pkg (brew has no Intel bottles for podman 6.x), podman machine init/start (2 cpu/4GB/30GB), load + tag localhost/yolo-jail:latest, run tests/test_jail.py + tests/test_runtime.py with YOLO_RUNTIME=podman and YOLO_TEST_JAIL_TIMEOUT=1200 (QEMU cold starts run 5-15 min)

pipelines.name: Justfile recipes (local dev pipeline)
pipelines.what: setup: editable `uv pip install -e .` into the project uv env (env -u VIRTUAL_ENV to ignore caller venv), then PATCHES the generated setuptools __editable___yolo_jail*_finder.py, replacing the static MAPPING line with code reading YOLO_REPO_ROOT env (fallback = repo abspath) — this single hack is what lets ONE editable install serve both the host checkout and the jail's /opt/yolo-jail mount. build: uv build. install: uv tool install --force wheel. deploy: install + legacy systemd-unit teardown + broker CA init (`yolo-claude-oauth-broker-host --init-ca`) + `yolo broker restart` so the singleton daemon picks up new code. build-image / build-image-minimal: nix build .#ociImage(.Minimal). load: ./result | $YOLO_RUNTIME load. cachix-push CACHE=yolo-jail: nix build --print-out-paths | cachix push, both variants. test: pytest tests/ (serial — session image build/load must not fork per worker). test-fast: -m 'not slow' -n 4 --dist worksteal. lint/lint-ci/format; check=format+lint+test-fast; check-ci=lint-ci+test-fast; check-all includes slow; clean

go_integration_points: flake.nix: add a `yoloGo = imagePkgs.buildGoModule { src = ./.; vendorHash = ...; }` (or per-binary subPackages) derivation and append it to corePackages, or replace the `entrypoint` writeTextFile wrapper with the Go binary directly. Two distinct target systems already exist in the flake: `pkgs` (host system, incl. aarch64-darwin) and `imagePkgs` (forced -linux). A Go entrypoint MUST be built with imagePkgs.buildGoModule so it is a Linux ELF; but note the current design deliberately avoids Linux builds on darwin hosts (entrypoint wrapper uses pkgs.writeTextFile precisely so no Linux builder is needed for repo-local derivations) — a natively-compiled Go entrypoint reintroduces a Linux-build requirement on Macs. Two mitigations: (a) Cachix publishes the closure (push-image-cache job already planned), (b) use Go's own cross-compilation (CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64) in a host-side derivation — trivially cross-compiles from darwin without a Linux builder, unlike C/Python; nixpkgs supports this via pkgsCross or a plain stdenv derivation running `go build` with GOOS/GOARCH set. Static (CGO_ENABLED=0) binaries also sidestep the image's glibc/LD_LIBRARY_PATH machinery entirely

entrypointPkg replacement: today `cp -r src/entrypoint -> $out/lib/python/entrypoint` + a bash wrapper exec'ing `python3 -m entrypoint`. Go version: ship one static binary at /bin/yolo-entrypoint; delete entrypointPkg + the PYTHONPATH wrapper. Seam is clean — the image's config only knows the path /bin/yolo-entrypoint; host-side run_cmd.py's preflight `import entrypoint` subprocess check (noted in the flake comment) would need a Go-equivalent preflight (e.g. `yolo-entrypoint --selfcheck`)

yoloCli in-jail wrapper: currently `uv run --with typer/rich/pyjson5 python3 -c 'from src.cli import main'` against /opt/yolo-jail. For a Go CLI the wrapper becomes `exec /opt/yolo-jail/bin/yolo-linux-$(uname -m)` (a committed or just-built artifact on the live mount) or the Nix-built Go binary baked into the image — but baking loses the live-edit property (see dev_loop). A hybrid: wrapper prefers /opt/yolo-jail/dist/yolo-go if present (dev), falls back to store path (release)

pyproject/console scripts: the 4 entry points (yolo, yolo-claude-oauth-broker-host, yolo-host-processes, yolo-ps) are the host-side seam. Incremental A/B: keep the Python wheel as the installer/shipping vehicle and have setup.py/hatch build hooks embed prebuilt Go binaries as package-data with thin Python `os.exec*` shims per entry point — flip one entry point at a time via env var (e.g. YOLO_USE_GO=yolo-ps) for parity testing. yolo-ps and yolo-host-processes are the smallest daemons and the natural first swaps. End state replaces PyPI wheel with: goreleaser/GitHub release binaries + brew formula from binaries (the poet-generated Python formula dies), or a wheel that's just a binary carrier (platform-tagged wheels: linux_x86_64, linux_aarch64, macosx_arm64 — publish.yml build job becomes a matrix or a cross-compile fan-out from one runner, easy with CGO_ENABLED=0)

Justfile: add `build-go` (go build ./cmd/... -> dist/ or bin/), make `install` depend on it, add gofmt/govet/golangci-lint to lint/lint-ci, `go test ./...` to test-fast. mise.toml ALREADY pins go = "latest" — toolchain provisioning is done. `just setup`'s editable-finder patch stays Python-only until the yolo CLI itself flips

Cross-compilation matrix: jail-image binaries (entrypoint, in-jail yolo, any shim replacements) need GOOS=linux GOARCH={amd64,arm64} (both CI arches, ubuntu-latest + ubuntu-24.04-arm build natively today); host binaries (yolo CLI, oauth broker, host-processes, yolo-ps) need linux/{amd64,arm64} + darwin/arm64 (+ darwin/amd64 while the Intel nightly runner exists — macos-26-intel is exercised until ~2027). All pure-Go (sockets, JSON, exec) so CGO_ENABLED=0 everywhere; one runner can emit the full matrix

buildGoModule specifics: vendorHash churn on every go.mod change is the main friction; either commit vendor/ (vendorHash = null) to keep `nix build --impure .#ociImageMinimal` reproducible in CI without hash bumps, or accept the two-step hash update. Because the flake is already evaluated with --impure (builtins.getEnv YOLO_EXTRA_PACKAGES), you could even read a prebuilt binary path from env in dev — but keep release images pure-source via buildGoModule. Also add the Go derivation to packages.* outputs so `nix build .#yoloEntrypointGo` gives a fast standalone build for tests, mirroring the existing packages.binPathLinks fast-path pattern

CI changes: check job gains setup-go (or mise) + go vet/test; build-image job unchanged in shape (nix builds the Go derivation inside the image build; add Go module proxy caching or vendoring to avoid network in nix sandbox — buildGoModule's fixed-output vendor drv handles this); publish.yml build job must produce per-platform artifacts instead of one pure wheel; brew job switches from homebrew-pypi-poet to a binary formula (url per-arch tarballs + sha256) — the current poet pipeline cannot express Go binaries; push-builder-image unaffected (builder image contains no repo Python)

Parity-test seam already in place: the CI integration job exercises the image end-to-end via tests/test_jail.py against `podman load`ed image and YOLO_RUNTIME env — swapping the entrypoint implementation inside the image is invisible to that suite, making it the primary A/B harness (run suite on python-entrypoint image vs go-entrypoint image, e.g. tag ci-minimal-go vs ci-minimal)
dev_loop: Today, host side: `just setup` does `uv pip install -e .` then patches the setuptools editable finder so MAPPING resolves src/ via $YOLO_REPO_ROOT at import time (fallback: the checkout path) — one editable install serves both the host repo and /opt/yolo-jail inside jails. In-jail: /workspace (the repo) also backs /opt/yolo-jail, and the image's /bin/yolo wrapper runs `uv run ... python3 -c 'from src.cli import main'` against that live mount — so edits to src/cli/* are live on the NEXT `yolo` invocation with zero build step, host and jail alike. Only src/entrypoint/ and flake.nix changes require `just load && just install` because entrypointPkg is baked into the Nix image (cp -r at image build). For released installs, `just install` = uv tool install of the wheel (decoupled from tree); `just deploy` additionally restarts the singleton broker so long-lived daemons pick up new code (stale-code-in-memory incident 2026-04-24 — a Go port of daemons has the same restart-on-deploy requirement, arguably worse since even the filesystem copy is stale until rebuilt). Go translation: the zero-build 'edit is live' property is lost for any ported unit — Go always needs a compile. Closest equivalents: (a) `go run ./cmd/yolo` in the in-jail wrapper (needs Go toolchain in the image or on the mount — mise.toml already pins go, and mise is in corePackages, so `mise x -- go run` works today at the cost of ~1s compile per invocation and GOCACHE in persistent ~/.cache); (b) a `just build-go` producing dist/bin/{linux-amd64,linux-arm64,darwin-arm64}/ into the live-mounted repo, with the in-jail wrapper exec'ing the arch-matching prebuilt — compile on host, run in jail instantly (cross-compile from darwin to linux is trivial with CGO_ENABLED=0, unlike the Python-era 'no Linux builder needed' constraint the flake works hard to preserve); (c) for the entrypoint the loop actually IMPROVES if the wrapper is taught a dev override (e.g. exec /opt/yolo-jail/dist/yolo-entrypoint if present) — today entrypoint edits force a full `just load` image rebuild, whereas a live-mounted rebuilt binary would not. Watch-mode (`just watch-go` via entr/air) can make (b) feel nearly as live as the Python loop.
notes: group=build-deploy. Key contract surfaces for parity: (1) YOLO_EXTRA_PACKAGES env var — JSON array read by flake via builtins.getEnv (hence every nix build is --impure); spec grammar: "name", "name.output" (single dot only, validator rejects more), {name,nixpkgs:rev}, {name,version,url,hash}, {name,outputs:[...]}; consumed by src/cli/image.py (sets build_env) and mirrored by the darwin path in src/cli/darwin_packages.py. (2) YOLO_RUNTIME env (default podman; 'container' for Apple Container) — Justfile `load` and CI. (3) YOLO_REPO_ROOT env — the editable-finder patch's dynamic root; jail sets it to /opt/yolo-jail. (4) YOLO_BUILDER_PUBKEY env — builder-image runtime key injection. (5) Image env contract the entrypoint re-exports: PATH shims-first, LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/lib:/usr/lib:/usr/lib/<arch>-linux-gnu, TZDIR, SSL_CERT_FILE, PKG_CONFIG_PATH, FONTCONFIG_*; /etc/ld.so.cache + /etc/localtime + /etc/timezone are symlinks into /run populated at STARTUP by the entrypoint (generate_ld_cache; must be reproduced by a Go entrypoint byte-for-byte in effect). (6) Flake output names are API: default/ociImage/ociImageMinimal/builderImage/binPathLinks/yoloDarwinPackages/darwinUnavailablePackages — consumed by Justfile, ci.yml, publish.yml, nightly-macos.yml, src/cli/image.py, src/cli/darwin_packages.py, src/cli/run_cmd.py (repo-root detection walks up looking for flake.nix; installed mode uses flake.nix+flake.lock bundled as wheel package-data via src/flake.nix -> ../flake.nix SYMLINKS — a Go-shipping story must keep shipping the flake files or teach the CLI a new source). (7) Wheel layout is itself an interface: src.bundled_loopholes/**/* ships loophole manifests; `just deploy` asserts yolo-claude-oauth-broker-host lands on PATH. Python-specific tricks that don't port 1:1: setuptools-scm git-tag versioning (Go: -ldflags -X main.version or runtime/debug ReadBuildInfo); the editable-finder MAPPING monkey-patch (no Go analog; replaced by 'rebuild into live mount'); `uv run --with typer --with rich` ad-hoc dep injection in the in-jail wrapper; homebrew-pypi-poet formula generation (Python-package-only tool); python3 -m entrypoint module invocation with relative-import-dependent PYTHONPATH layout. Version pins that matter: python313 in flake (both entrypoint interpreter and in-jail uv python), requires-python >=3.13, deps typer/rich/pyjson5 (pyjson5 = JSONC config parsing — Go needs a JSON5/JSONC parser with identical acceptance for yolo-jail.jsonc parity). swarf/: a scratch/working-notes directory — resolved open questions from the open-sourcing effort (naming, license, install method, repo setup) plus research docs, plans (isolation audit, pinned package versions, cross-project roadmap), and task notes; no build inputs live there. scripts/README.md documents that the old systemd claude-token-refresher is gone (broker refreshes on demand under flock); scripts/ contributes nothing to build. Files: /workspace/flake.nix, /workspace/Justfile, /workspace/pyproject.toml, /workspace/mise.toml (pins node 22, python 3.13, go latest, uv), /workspace/.github/workflows/{ci,publish,nightly-macos}.yml, /workspace/scripts/README.md, /workspace/src/flake.nix and /workspace/src/flake.lock (symlinks to root, wheel-shipped).
